state.output:
<path>/mnt/c/Users/jonad/Audio-Generation/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Audio Recorder & Playback System with AI Agents + MCP
2: 
3: End-to-end audio platform with:
4: 
5: - FastAPI backend (JWT auth, upload, metadata, streaming)
6: - PostgreSQL + async SQLAlchemy
7: - React frontend (recording, playback, search, AI assistant UI)
8: - MCP server exposing recording intelligence tools
9: - AI agent orchestration for multi-step reasoning over recordings
10: - CLI acceptance tests + AI CLI commands
11: 
12: ## Table of Contents
13: 
14: 1. System Overview
15: 2. Architecture
16: 3. Project Structure
17: 4. Core Features
18: 5. AI Features
19: 6. MCP Server
20: 7. Agent Orchestration
21: 8. API Reference
22: 9. Database & Migrations
23: 10. Setup Guide
24: 11. CLI Usage
25: 12. Security & Safety
26: 13. Troubleshooting
27: 14. Next Improvements
28: 
29: ## 1) System Overview
30: 
31: The system allows authenticated users to:
32: 
33: - Record/upload audio
34: - Store audio files and metadata
35: - List, play, and delete recordings
36: - Transcribe recordings
37: - Search recordings semantically (vector search)
38: - Summarize recordings
39: - Ask questions across recording transcripts
40: 
41: ## 2) Architecture
42: 
43: ### Backend
44: 
45: - Framework: FastAPI
46: - Auth: JWT bearer tokens
47: - ORM: SQLAlchemy async
48: - DB: PostgreSQL + pgvector
49: - AI service layer:
50:   - transcription
51:   - embeddings
52:   - summarization
53:   - question answering
54: 
55: ### Frontend
56: 
57: - React + TypeScript
58: - Audio capture via `MediaRecorder`
59: - Dashboard playback/list/search
60: - AI assistant chat panel with reasoning steps
61: 
62: ### AI/MCP
63: 
64: - MCP server (Python MCP SDK) provides structured tools
65: - Agent orchestrates tool calls based on user query intent
66: - Backend `/agent/query` also provides API-based orchestration with step traces
67: 
68: ## 3) Project Structure
69: 
70: ```text
71: agent/                         # MCP-connected agent orchestration
72: backend/
73:   app/
74:     api/routes/                # auth, recordings, agent routes
75:     core/                      # config, database, security
76:     models/                    # SQLAlchemy models
77:     schemas/                   # Pydantic schemas
78:     services/                  # auth, recording, AI, agent services
79:   migrations/                  # SQL migration for AI extensions
80:   API_CONTRACT_AI.md           # AI endpoint contracts
81: cli/
82:   test_api.py                  # acceptance tests
83:   ai_cli.py                    # AI-focused CLI commands
84: frontend/
85:   src/
86:     pages/Dashboard.tsx        # recorder + recordings + assistant UI
87:     services/api.ts            # frontend API client
88: mcp_server/
89:   server.py                    # MCP tool server
90: ```
91: 
92: ## 4) Core Features
93: 
94: Already supported:
95: 
96: - User registration/login
97: - Audio upload
98: - File storage under `uploads/audio/<user_id>/...`
99: - Metadata persistence
100: - Recordings list
101: - Recording stream endpoint
102: - Delete recording
103: 
104: ## 5) AI Features
105: 
106: ### Data model extension
107: 
108: `AudioRecording` includes:
109: 
110: - `transcript` (`TEXT`)
111: - `transcript_embedding` (`VECTOR(1536)`)
112: 
113: ### AI endpoints
114: 
115: - `POST /recordings/{id}/transcribe`
116: - `POST /recordings/search`
117: - `POST /recordings/{id}/summarize`
118: - `POST /recordings/answer`
119: - `POST /agent/query`
120: 
121: Detailed payloads are documented in:
122: - [backend/API_CONTRACT_AI.md](C:/Users/jonad/Audio-Generation/backend/API_CONTRACT_AI.md)
123: 
124: ### Provider routing model
125: 
126: The backend supports split providers per capability:
127: 
128: - transcription provider
129: - embedding provider
130: - chat provider
131: 
132: Each can use separate `API_KEY`, `BASE_URL`, and `MODEL`, with fallback to `OPENAI_*`.
133: 
134: ## 6) MCP Server
135: 
136: MCP tool server:
137: - [mcp_server/server.py](C:/Users/jonad/Audio-Generation/mcp_server/server.py)
138: 
139: Tools:
140: 
141: - `list_recordings(user_id, token?)`
142: - `get_recording_metadata(recording_id, token?)`
143: - `transcribe_audio(recording_id, token?)`
144: - `summarize_audio(recording_id, token?)`
145: - `search_recordings(query, limit?, token?)`
146: - `answer_question_about_recordings(question, limit?, token?)`
147: 
148: All tool inputs/outputs are structured and JWT-scoped through backend APIs.
149: 
150: ## 7) Agent Orchestration
151: 
152: ### MCP-connected agent module
153: 
154: - [agent/mcp_agent.py](C:/Users/jonad/Audio-Generation/agent/mcp_agent.py)
155: 
156: Supported patterns:
157: 
158: - "Show my latest recording"
159: - "Summarize my latest recording"
160: - "Find recordings mentioning deadlines"
161: - "What did I say about the meeting?"
162: 
163: ### Backend API agent endpoint
164: 
165: - `POST /agent/query`
166: - Returns:
167:   - final `answer`
168:   - list of `steps` with tools + previews
169: 
170: ## 8) API Reference (quick)
171: 
172: ### Auth
173: 
174: - `POST /auth/register`
175: - `POST /auth/login`
176: 
177: ### Recordings
178: 
179: - `POST /recordings/upload`
180: - `GET /recordings`
181: - `GET /recordings/{id}`
182: - `DELETE /recordings/{id}`
183: - `GET /recordings/{id}/stream`
184: 
185: ### AI
186: 
187: - `POST /recordings/{id}/transcribe`
188: - `POST /recordings/search`
189: - `POST /recordings/{id}/summarize`
190: - `POST /recordings/answer`
191: - `POST /agent/query`
192: 
193: ## 9) Database & Migrations
194: 
195: Migration file:
196: 
197: - [backend/migrations/001_ai_extensions.sql](C:/Users/jonad/Audio-Generation/backend/migrations/001_ai_extensions.sql)
198: 
199: What it does:
200: 
201: - enables `vector` extension
202: - adds transcript fields
203: - adds ivfflat vector index
204: 
205: ## 10) Setup Guide
206: 
207: ### Prerequisites
208: 
209: - Python 3.12+
210: - Node.js 18+
211: - PostgreSQL with `pgvector`
212: 
213: ### Backend
214: 
215: ```powershell
216: cd backend
217: python -m venv venv
218: .\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
219: pip install -r requirements.txt
220: ```
221: 
222: Create `backend/.env` from `.env.example`.
223: 
224: Run migration:
225: 
226: ```sql
227: -- execute backend/migrations/001_ai_extensions.sql
228: ```
229: 
230: Start API:
231: 
232: ```powershell
233: uvicorn app.main:app --reload
234: ```
235: 
236: ### Frontend
237: 
238: ```powershell
239: cd frontend
240: npm install
241: npm start
242: ```
243: 
244: ### MCP Server
245: 
246: ```powershell
247: cd mcp_server
248: python -m venv .venv
249: .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
250: pip install -r requirements.txt
251: python server.py
252: ```
253: 
254: ## 11) CLI Usage
255: 
256: ### Acceptance test suite
257: 
258: ```powershell
259: cd cli
260: python test_api.py run-tests
261: ```
262: 
263: ### AI commands
264: 
265: ```powershell
266: cd cli
267: python ai_cli.py transcribe-recording 42 --email you@example.com
268: python ai_cli.py summarize-recording 42 --email you@example.com
269: python ai_cli.py search-recordings "deadlines" --email you@example.com
270: python ai_cli.py ask-agent "What did I say about project deadlines?" --user-id 1 --email you@example.com
271: ```
272: 
273: ## 12) Security & Safety
274: 
275: Implemented controls:
276: 
277: - JWT enforcement for all user recording access
278: - user-scoped DB queries (`recording.user_id == current_user.id`)
279: - prompt-injection pattern blocking for AI query fields
280: - input sanitization + max length limits
281: - structured tool inputs/outputs in MCP
282: 
283: Additional recommended hardening:
284: 
285: - rate limiting per user for AI endpoints
286: - audit log for AI tool invocations
287: - strict MIME/codec checks on upload
288: - background jobs + retries for transcription
289: 
290: ## 13) Troubleshooting
291: 
292: ### `pgvector` not found
293: 
294: - Ensure PostgreSQL extension is installed and run:
295:   - `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;`
296: 
297: ### Transcription fails with OpenRouter
298: 
299: - Some providers may not support `/audio/transcriptions` the same way.
300: - Use split provider config:
301:   - chat via OpenRouter
302:   - transcription/embedding via OpenAI-compatible endpoint with those APIs.
303: 
304: ### `EMBEDDING_API_KEY (or OPENAI_API_KEY fallback) is not configured`
305: 
306: - Set at least one key in `backend/.env`:
307:   - `OPENAI_API_KEY=<your_key>` (shared fallback for all AI capabilities), or
308:   - `EMBEDDING_API_KEY=<your_key>` (embedding-only override)
309: - Restart the backend after editing `.env`.
310: 
311: ## 14) Next Improvements
312: 
313: 1. Replace raw SQL migrations with Alembic migration pipeline.
314: 2. Add async background worker for transcription/embedding jobs.
315: 3. Add integration tests for AI endpoints and agent flows.
316: 4. Add token usage/cost tracking for AI operations.

(End of file - total 316 lines)
</content>
state.title: README.md

metadata.preview:
# Audio Recorder & Playback System with AI Agents + MCP

End-to-end audio platform with:

- FastAPI backend (JWT auth, upload, metadata, streaming)
- PostgreSQL + async SQLAlchemy
- React frontend (recording, playback, search, AI assistant UI)
- MCP server exposing recording intelligence tools
- AI agent orchestration for multi-step reasoning over recordings
- CLI acceptance tests + AI CLI commands

## Table of Contents

1. System Overview
2. Architecture
3. Project Structure
4. Core Features
5. AI Features
6. MCP Server
7. Agent Orchestration

parts.text: The user asked me to read the README.md file. I've done that. Now I should just provide a concise summary of what it contains without unnecessary preamble.

This is an audio recording and playback system with AI capabilities. It includes:

- **Backend**: FastAPI with JWT auth, PostgreSQL + pgvector for storage
- **Frontend**: React for recording, playback, search, and AI assistant UI
- **MCP Server**: Exposes recording intelligence tools
- **AI Features**: Transcription, semantic search, summarization, Q&A over transcripts

Key features: audio upload/streaming, transcription, vector search, AI agent orchestration, CLI acceptance tests.

The README covers setup (Python 3.12+, Node.js 18+, PostgreSQL with pgvector), project structure, API endpoints, and usage instructions.
